title: Rainbow Mergirl and the Kelp Forest Thief
summary: When Rainbow Mergirl discovers her kelp forest has been devoured down to the stubs, she suspects the local Kelpbasses. Alongside the dwarf Seeker, she encounters a struggling Kelpbass gasping for water in a dry castle. After saving the fish by bringing him to her water-filled home, Rainbow Mergirl realizes his thievery stems from a lack of hands to grow his own food. In a heartwarming conclusion, she reveals that she has planted a massive kelp farm in the fish's own backyard, ensuring he stays full and her garden stays safe.

highlightQuote.text: I'm Rainbow Mergirl says, um, so I have a kelp forest in my front yard and and today I was going to water it, but it's gone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There's the start. Are you listening to that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to be a Mergirl in this problem line.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A Mergirl problem? What kind of a problem do you think?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding dong ding da... it's Rainbow Mergirl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, come on in, Rainbow Mergirl. I haven't seen you in a while.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm Rainbow Mergirl says, um, so I have a kelp forest in my front yard and and today I was going to water it, but it's gone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Gone? What do you mean gone?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I mean gone. My kelp forest is gone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Like someone chewed off some of the leaves?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, like someone chewed off the whole kelp forest.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All the way down to the ground?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Did they pull the roots out?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't think they pulled the roots out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so there's little stubs left in the sea floor?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well that's terrible news, Rainbow Mergirl. Who who do you think would do a thing like that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Kelpbasses.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the Kelpbasses. Now we've met them before. Ding ding ding. Didn't they eat up an entire kelp forest in your backyard once before?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ugh. And they're back at it again. What should we do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ah, I know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Get a book about them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I wouldn't have thought of about that idea, Seeker, but I do love getting books. Which library do you want to go to?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I guess I got one today. Oh wait, there's a Kelpbass behind me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There there it is, Seeker. How did you get into my house, Kelpbass?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And what does the Kelpbass say?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Um... [coughs] excuse me. Because the Kelpbass was coughing. And Seeker said, 'Oh my goodness, Kelpbass, are you okay?' And the Kelpbass said, '[coughs] I can't... [coughs].'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What's wrong, Seeker? Rainbow Mergirl, do you know what's wrong with this Kelpbass?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Kelpbass... I can't... Oh, what is it? What?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here, here, here, Kelpbass, here's a book about you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, I can't... No, that's... Oh, that is a very interesting book, but I can't read it right now because... [coughs]. And the Kelpbass flopped over on its side and its gills were opening and closing, but there was no water because they were in Seeker's living room in Seeker's castle.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh. And Rainbow Mergirl said, 'What about we swim to my castle?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, please, Daisy. Help. I just need some water to breathe.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Or at my house, or at my house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, how did they get to Rainbow Mergirl's house?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They just, it's just ten yards away, it's just five yards away.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so they walked there? Well, but the Kelpbass can't walk there.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, Kelpbass swam there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But there's no water.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, no, no. At her, Rainbow Mergirl's house, there was water.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. There was water inside her living room.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Kelpbass said, 'Oh my goodness, whew, that was a close one there, Seeker. Glub, glub, glub. Your house is way too dry.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, 'Well, I am a dwarf, you see, a kind of a gnome person and we breathe the air.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Air, disgusting! Wouldn't you just want to swim in a beautiful cool water all the time, like this wonderful Mergirl has in her house? It's so lovely and you can swim around this way and that and up and down and then if there's ever anything nice you want to nibble on, you can just nibble on it all day. Nibble, nibble, nibble, nibble.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, 'Oh yes...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But Rainbow Mergirl said, 'You ate my kelp forest!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I did? Oh dear.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think so.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, can I get you a new kelp forest?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, what could we do about that? Well, maybe I just have to show you. Maybe I just need to talk to you about if if you pulled out the roots. Kelpbass, did you pull out the roots?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Roots? No, I just nibbled the nice leafy parts, you see? Those are the delicious parts of the kelp, the leaves, the leaves, you know, they sway in the water and they're nice and soft and oh, I could use some kelp right... Do you have any kelp? I could use some kelp right now.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, there's some fresh kelp from yesterday in my refrigerator.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I'll take some. And Seeker said, 'Kelpbass, you're just eating all of Rainbow Mergirl's kelp. You must be just very hungry all the time.' And Kelpbass said, 'Yes, I'm a big, big fish, we gotta eat a lot of kelp.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, Rainbow Mergirl said, 'Can you swim back to your house because yesterday when you were at the kelp store, I planted something for you in your backyard.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You did? Said Kelpbass.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's just, it's a whole farm of kelp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A whole farm of kelp just for me?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Just for you. See, I'm just a fish, I don't have arms like you do, Rainbow Mergirl, or legs like you do, Seeker. So I can't really do any farming, I can't grow my own kelp, that's why I wander around eating other people's kelp which is so delicious. [Eating sounds] and he gobbles up Rainbow Mergirl's refrigerator and ate that too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And and now Kelpbass, can you go to your house and see and see the farm of kelp?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Kelpbass went back to his house and saw the incredibly beautiful farm that Rainbow Mergirl had planted just for him and he said, 'Oh thanks, now I won't need to eat your kelp forest because I've got my own.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And that's the end of the story.
